preset_description: Dark = decision signal. Emphasis accumulates toward conversion — trust anchors and commitment moments are dark, top-of-funnel is light.

brief.audience: SaaS trial users for AI-first, semantic design system

accent_tokens._note: Brand primary CTA color. Global — not per-preset. Two variants: button on a light/neutral section vs. on a dark section.

sections.name: Global Navigation
sections.rationale: Every page begins with NavigationHeader. For a Validator at the decision stage, use the minimal variant to reduce distraction and keep a persistent 'Upgrade Now' CTA visible throughout the session — this is the highest-converting element on most SaaS pages and directly serves decision-stage intent.

sections.name: Hero — Value Reaffirmation
sections.rationale: Trial users at the decision stage already know the product; the hero must reaffirm the value they've experienced and frame the upgrade as the logical next step. HeroPrimary's Validator variant includes a trust_signal (e.g., 'Trusted by 2,400+ design teams') which anchors credibility above the fold — critical for Validators who need external proof before committing. The with-social-proof sub-variant places a LogoStrip immediately below the CTA cluster, compressing social proof into the first viewport.

sections.name: Social Proof — Credibility Anchor
sections.rationale: Validators at the decision stage weigh peer validation heavily. SocialProofBar's Validator variant leads with a featured quote from a recognizable customer, with logos below — this stacks narrative proof on top of brand-recognition proof. Placing it immediately after the hero sustains trust momentum before the page asks the user to evaluate specifics.

sections.name: Outcome Metrics — Quantified Impact
sections.rationale: StatBlock serves decision-stage Validators by translating product value into verifiable, outcome-oriented numbers (e.g., '47% faster design-to-dev handoff — Source: 2024 Customer Survey'). Source attribution is mandatory for this archetype; unsourced stats reduce trust. Positioned after social proof, it transitions the narrative from 'others trust us' to 'here is the measurable impact.'

sections.name: Capability Proof — Feature-to-Benefit Mapping
sections.rationale: FeatureGrid at the decision stage addresses the Validator's need to confirm that paid-tier capabilities justify the cost. Outcome-oriented benefit statements (not feature labels) align with Validator reasoning patterns. Use the 3-col-icon layout for 6 or 9 benefits, ensuring each item maps a feature to a concrete workflow outcome the trial user has likely encountered.

sections.name: Decision-Point Testimonial
sections.rationale: TestimonialSingle is purpose-built for the Validator archetype at the decision stage. A single, deeply attributed testimonial (name, role, company, photo, logo) placed adjacent to the pricing section acts as the final trust signal before the conversion moment. The quote should address the trial-to-paid transition specifically — e.g., 'We upgraded after two weeks and never looked back.' with-photo and with-company-logo variants maximize perceived authenticity.

sections.name: Pricing — Plan Comparison
sections.rationale: PricingCard is the core decision-stage conversion mechanism. The Validator variant includes descriptions and extended feature lists so users can self-validate their plan choice. Use the highlighted variant on the recommended tier and with-comparison for enterprise differentiation. This section must answer every remaining objection a Validator has before clicking 'Upgrade.'

sections.name: Terminal CTA — Conversion Capture
sections.rationale: CTABanner closes the argument. The Validator variant includes a reassuring subhead and trust_signal ('No long-term contract · Cancel anytime · SOC 2 compliant') to neutralize last-moment friction at the decision stage. Use the split layout — directional copy on the left summarizing the upgrade promise, CTA on the right — to give Validators one final rational frame before acting.

page.reasoning: minimal variant for conversion-focused pages — strip nav_links, keep logo and single CTA only. Decision-stage Validators need focused wayfinding with persistent CTA.
Validator variant always includes trust_signal. with-social-proof variant places LogoStrip immediately below CTACluster, establishing credibility at the decision moment.

slots.headline: Your trial proved it. Paid unlocks the rest.

page.reasoning: Validator variant leads with quote, logos below. This combination maximizes trust signal for trial-to-paid conversion at decision stage.

slots.headline: What teams say after making the switch
slots.quote: We ran MODE on trial for three weeks. The moment we upgraded, our token pipeline went from a two-day sync to under 20 minutes. The numbers alone justified the seat cost.
slots.attribution: Priya Nair, Head of Design Systems · Lattice

page.reasoning: Always pair stats with source attribution in Validator contexts — unsourced stats reduce trust. 3-stat is the preferred default and supports decision-making credibility.

stats.label: Faster design-to-dev handoff after upgrading from trial

Average onboarding time for new paid-tier team members

page.reasoning: Validator benefits from specific, outcome-oriented descriptions — not feature names, benefit statements. 3-col-icon is the default and aligns with decision-stage validation needs.

slots.headline: What you unlock when you upgrade
slots.subhead: Every paid-tier capability maps directly to a workflow problem you've already hit in trial.

features.title: Semantic Token Engine
features.description: Resolve token conflicts automatically — teams report 60% fewer handoff revision cycles after enabling semantic resolution. (2024 customer survey)

features.title: Real-Time Sync
features.description: Push design decisions to code in under 60 seconds, eliminating the manual export step that trial users flag as their top bottleneck. (Internal data, Q1 2025)

features.title: Role-Based Access Control
features.description: Enforce contributor vs. consumer permissions across every workspace — zero accidental overwrites, full audit trail.

features.title: Usage Analytics Dashboard
features.description: See exactly which components and tokens your team adopts, so you can deprecate dead weight and double down on what ships.

features.title: Multi-Brand Theming
features.description: Maintain up to 10 brand themes from a single token source — paid teams cut per-brand maintenance time by 38%. (2024 customer survey)

features.title: Priority Integrations
features.description: Native connectors to Figma, GitHub, Storybook, and Jira — configured in one setup flow, not three separate plugin installs.

page.reasoning: with-photo increases engagement and perceived authenticity. Always include role and company for Validator credibility. Place near a CTA — testimonials convert when positioned at a decision moment.

slots.quote: We upgraded after two weeks and never second-guessed it. The semantic layer alone saved our lead engineer four hours a week on token QA. At our headcount, that's a real number — and it compounds every sprint.

page.reasoning: Validator variant includes description and longer feature list. highlighted variant for recommended plan drives conversion at decision stage.

slots.description: Everything trial users need to take their design system to production — with the token depth, sync speed, and access controls that make the upgrade pay for itself.
features: Role-based access control with audit log
Priority email and Slack support (< 4 hr response)

page.reasoning: Validator variant: include subhead and trust_signal (e.g., 'No credit card required'). Centered placement with trust signal directly captures conversion intent.
